title: To Flush, My Dog by Elizabeth Barrett Browning — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Yet, my pretty sportive friend,
Little is't to such an end
That I praise thy rareness!
Other dogs may be thy peers
Haply in these drooping ears,
And this glossy fairness.

But of thee it shall be said,
meaning: "To Flush, My Dog" by Elizabeth Barrett Browning is a love and deep and nature poem consisting of 48 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Yet, my pretty sportive friend, Little is't to such an end...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Elizabeth Barrett Browning's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
